title: Desmanthus palmeri
taxonomy: Desmanthus palmeri is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flower head structure and leaf morphology. Desmanthus palmeri is further categorized within the subfamily Asteroideae, which includes a wide range of genera and species. The genus Desmanthus itself is part of the tribe Astereae, a group of plants that share similar characteristics. Within the tribe Astereae, Desmanthus palmeri is often placed alongside other similar species.

side_effects: Desmanthus palmeri, also known as Palmer's snakeweed, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, but its side effects are not well-documented. However, some reports suggest that excessive consumption of the plant may cause gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The plant's high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones may also be toxic to the liver and kidneys if ingested in large amounts. intro: Desmanthus palmeri, also known as Palmer's snakeweed, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Desmanthus palmeri include flavonoids, terpenoids, and caffeic acid derivatives, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
